code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate return
    - Safety hazards or product failure within 1 month
    - Explicit declarations: "terrible," "junk," "don't buy"
    - Time-sensitive failures: "Broke within X weeks/months"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite some positives
    - Multiple quality issues affecting daily use
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with no clear winner
    - Conditional utility: "good for occasional use"
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deductions for usability/durability issues
    - Functional excellence with specific minor drawbacks
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still recommend"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Praise of multiple aspects (functionality, design, ease of use) without drawbacks
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" declarations override critiques
    - Multi-feature praise outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "exceeded expectations"
    - Deductions with strong recommendation: "Deduct 1 star but still love"

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Multi-aspect praise with no criticisms →5
    - Explicit overall ratings override aspect-specific scores ("5 stars overall")
    - Update sections prioritize final evaluation over initial experience
    - Safety issues override all positives →1 regardless of positive remarks
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" phrasing →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make it exceptional!
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite specific critique. Strong endorsement confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "I'm deducting one star because assembly was tricky, but this is still a 5-star product! Works perfectly once set up."
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides minor critique. Enthusiastic conclusion.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Solves my jar-scraping problem perfectly! Deducting one star because it isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback. Usability impact warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: The blender makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: This blender works perfectly and looks stunning! Blends everything smoothly while enhancing my kitchen's style.
<Reasoning>Praises both functionality and aesthetics without criticisms. Clear enthusiastic endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Deducting one star for lid design, but this is revolutionary! Changed how I cook forever."
<Reasoning>Strong transformative praise outweighs minor design critique. Emotional endorsement warrants 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "The towels look luxurious but require 3 washes to become absorbent. Deducting one star for inconvenience."
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction without overriding endorsement. Functional deficiency warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Catastrophic failure after 1 week. Dangerous sparks - immediate return!
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides any potential positives. Clear 1-star scenario.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 12
  gold 4 -> 5: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "My wife and I recently got talked into a camping weekend, and as neither of us has camped in decades, we wanted to do so in a bit of luxury.  I'm a rather large dude, and I've laid on enough beds that lost all of their air to know I wanted a good bed..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "does exactly what I need it to do. Not too thick and fits well. has deep pockets to fit about any thickness of mattress"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
